docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Jeremiah Michaels
docs.text:
I have been a technical writer for over 15 years, focusing on SaaS applications in the VMS and Fintech spaces.

I have extensive experience working alongside developers, product managers, QA, and even sales. My career started in journalism. My time was spent chasing facts, and talking to people to get them. My natural love of technology led me into a techincal writing career. I can learn what I need to, and fill knowledge gaps with interviews and the internet. Then I write the docs.

When I'm not behind a screen, I am a typical Californian, spending as much time as I can outside. I enjoy just about any aspect of nature.

docs.title: Idea
docs.text:
The idea for the file-api is to showcase my API documentation abilities. I used claude desktop to code a locally hosted API that I could generate an OpenAPI spec from and deploy with the Swagger UI.

I wanted a secure location since the API is exposed to the internet.

docs.title: Frameworks
docs.text: I used python and fastapi for the framework. I wanted something that was easy to create and deploy. fastapi create a yml file by default.

docs.title: Prompt
docs.text:
I first prompted claude desktop about what my goal was. I asked what would be the easiest to do, and why. I was presented with three options, but the end of the output suggested that I use the python \ fastapi combo since it accomplished my goals and was the most straightforward.

Then I prompted my ideas for two functions, one to download a txt file from a folder, with the folder name as a required input parameter. The second function provides a list of files in a folder, with the folder name as a required parameter.

I made sure to add that I wanted the highest level of security since I would be testing the endpoints on my local when it's connected to the internet.
